channel.title: Home on Mike Wilcome’s Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Home on Mike Wilcome’s Blog

item.title: Building Backend Pipelines for Comporium: Taming the Sales Beast
item.description:
Diving into Comporium’s Southeast Empire

Back at Orange Bees, I took on a gig with Comporium, a Southeast staple slinging phone, internet, TV, and security services across the Carolinas. My mission? Build and support a fleet of Java microservices to power their sales pipelines—an internal tool that sales reps lean on to wrestle orders into shape. This wasn’t just coding; it was about streamlining a tangled mess into something sleek, all while juggling the ins and outs of their massive product lineup.

item.title: Supporting Michelin FixPix Backend: Roadside Rescue Done Right
item.description:
Hitting the Road with Michelin

From August 2022 to December 2023, I got my hands dirty at Orange Bees, powering up the backend for Michelin FixPix—a slick little app tied into Michelin’s OnCall roadside assistance gig. This wasn’t just a job; it was a full-on challenge that had me wrestling with Java, Apache Camel, Vaadin, Velocity, and Spring to make tire service workflows hum like a well-tuned engine. Rewarding? You bet—seeing it come together felt like nailing a clutch pit stop.

item.title: Sync My Health Data: Wearables for Veterans
item.description:
Turning Wearables into Lifelines

From April 2019 to August 2022, I stepped up as the lead architect for Sync My Health Data—a beast of a project that’s honestly one of my proudest wins. Picture this: I built a backend API that ties together 20+ health apps and 800+ devices, pulling live data straight to VA doctors. We’re talking heavy hitters like Fitbit and Garmin, plus a slew of others, all feeding into a system I crafted with Java and Server-Sent Events (SSE). The mission? Deliver real-time insights on heart health, diabetes, and fitness for veterans—data that’s making a difference every day.

item.title: Architecting VBMS Correspondence Web Service: From Chaos to Clarity
item.description:
Facing the Monolith Monster

Back at Booz Allen Hamilton, from September 2015 to March 2019, I took on a beast of a project: the VBMS Correspondence Web Service. When I started, it was a mess—20+ modules tangled up in a hulking monolithic app, part of an even bigger system for the Veterans Affairs. My mission? Rip it apart, rebuild it, and make it work—really work—for vets needing their benefits. Over the years, I led the charge to split it out into its own service, rewriting it as a lean Jersey and Spring Boot microservice, with a sprinkle of Groovy for good measure. The result? A streamlined powerhouse that churned out formatted PDF documents from requests, cutting through the VA’s correspondence chaos.

item.title: About Me
item.description:
Mike Wilcome

Principal Engineer | Full-Stack Java Expert | Tech Leader

I’m Mike Wilcome, a Principal Engineer passionate about building innovative software solutions. With expertise in full-stack Java development, microservices, and leadership, I’ve spent over a decade delivering impactful projects in tech, healthcare, and veteran services.

Education

Bachelor of Science in Computer Science
Charleston Southern University
2009 - 2014
Learned the foundations of software engineering while honing skills in leadership and service.
